---
title: "CGM QC Features"
author: "Tyler Tong"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: false
engine: knitr
---

# Load packages and files
```{R}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(gt)
library(purrr)
library(janitor)
library(jsonlite)
library(lubridate)
cgm <- fromJSON("~/AiReadi/AiReadiSummer2026/Data/mini/dexcom_g6/1029/1029_DEX.json", simplifyDataFrame = TRUE, flatten = TRUE)
```

```{R}

# create new header

uuid <- cgm$header$uuid
timezone <- cgm$header$timezone
schema_name <- cgm$header$schema_id$name
newheader <- list(uuid = uuid, timezone = timezone, schema_name = schema_name)

# clean cgm readings

cgm_readings <- cgm$body$cgm |> as_tibble()
cgm_readings <- filter(cgm_readings, event_type == "EGV", .preserve = TRUE)
cgm_readings <- select(cgm_readings, 
                       event_type, 
                       effective_time_frame.time_interval.start_date_time, 
                       blood_glucose.unit, 
                       blood_glucose.value)

# get location based on timezone

if(newheader$timezone == "pst" | newheader$timezone == "pdt")
  newheader$location <- "America/Los_Angeles"
if(newheader$timezone == "mst" | newheader$timezone == "mdt")
  newheader$location <- "America/Denver"
if(newheader$timezone == "cst" | newheader$timezone == "cdt")
  newheader$location <- "America/Chicago"
if(newheader$timezone == "est" | newheader$timezone == "edt")
  newheader$location <- "America/New_York"

# get datetime instead of char

cgm_readings <- mutate(cgm_readings, datetime = ymd_hms(effective_time_frame.time_interval.start_date_time))

# convert to local time

cgm_readings <- mutate(cgm_readings, dt_local = 
                         if_else(str_sub(effective_time_frame.time_interval.start_date_time, -1, -1) == "Z", 
                       with_tz(time = datetime, tzone = newheader$location), 
                       datetime
                       ))
cgm_readings <- select(cgm_readings, !datetime)

# remove duplicates

cgm_readings <- distinct(cgm_readings)

# get unusual cgms and remove from dataset

unusual_cgm_readings <- cgm_readings |> 
  filter(blood_glucose.value > 400 | blood_glucose.value < 40, .preserve = TRUE)
normal_cgm_readings <- anti_join(cgm_readings, unusual_cgm_readings, by = "dt_local")

# create reading-level table

reading_cgm <- select(normal_cgm_readings, blood_glucose.value, dt_local)
head(reading_cgm)

# count days

daycounts <- count(normal_cgm_readings, date(dt_local))
head(daycounts)

# remove invalid days

normal_cgm_readings <- mutate(normal_cgm_readings, "date(dt_local)" = date(dt_local))
joined <- left_join(normal_cgm_readings, daycounts, by = "date(dt_local)" )
joined <- filter(joined, n >= 202)
normal_cgm_readings <- select(joined, !n)
normal_cgm_readings <- select(normal_cgm_readings, !`date(dt_local)`)

# create summary table

validdaycounts <- filter(daycounts, n >= 202)
summary <- list(num_readings = nrow(normal_cgm_readings), 
                num_days = nrow(validdaycounts), 
                mean_cgm = mean(normal_cgm_readings$blood_glucose.value, na.rm = TRUE),
                median_cgm = median(normal_cgm_readings$blood_glucose.value, na.rm = TRUE),
                sd_cgm = sd(normal_cgm_readings$blood_glucose.value, na.rm = TRUE),
                CV_cgm = (sd(normal_cgm_readings$blood_glucose.value, na.rm = TRUE)/
                            mean(normal_cgm_readings$blood_glucose.value, na.rm = TRUE)) * 100,
                medium_cgm = sum((
                  normal_cgm_readings$blood_glucose.value > 70 & 
                    normal_cgm_readings$blood_glucose.value < 180), na.rm = TRUE) * 100/
                  nrow(normal_cgm_readings),
                low_cgm = sum((normal_cgm_readings$blood_glucose.value < 70), na.rm = TRUE) * 100/
                  nrow(normal_cgm_readings),
                high_cgm = sum((normal_cgm_readings$blood_glucose.value > 180), na.rm = TRUE) * 100/
                  nrow(normal_cgm_readings))

summary

```
